notes: Apache Fluss (incubating) is a Flink-native streaming storage layer for real-time lakehouses, tiering data into Iceberg or Paimon. The name is the German word for "river" and the project README states it is pronounced /flus/ — a short German u, closest to English "floos" — so it is not English "fuss", and the double-s stays voiceless rather than turning into a z.
